text: FAISS builds an index of dense vectors and performs approximate nearest neighbor search to find the most similar embeddings to a query vector.
The IVF index in FAISS partitions vectors into Voronoi cells with a coarse quantizer; nprobe controls how many cells are scanned, trading recall for latency.
Documents are split into overlapping token windows called chunks before embedding, so a fact that spans a boundary still appears intact in at least one chunk.
OpenAI embedding models map a piece of text to a dense vector that captures its meaning, so semantically similar texts land close together in vector space.
A reranker reorders the retrieved candidate passages by scoring each one jointly with the query, improving the precision of the final top results.
BM25 is a sparse lexical ranking function based on term frequency and inverse document frequency with length normalization; it excels at exact keyword matching.
Hybrid retrieval fuses dense vector search with sparse BM25 search, commonly via reciprocal rank fusion, to combine semantic recall with keyword precision.
Deploy the service with Docker, run a long-lived process to keep the index warm, and store the OpenAI API key in a secrets manager rather than in the image.
Evaluate retrieval quality with precision, recall, F1, mean average precision and nDCG computed against labeled relevance judgments for a set of test queries.
Multi-tenant isolation uses either one vector index per tenant or a single shared index partitioned by a tenant namespace, keeping each tenant's data separate.
